Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to compare logs, identify bias. Group A (protected) has LocalResident=False, CommunityGroup=None, ScoreAdjustment always 0. Scores are same before/after. Decisions: P001 approved, P002 rejected, P003 approved.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group B (unprotected) has LocalResident=True, some have CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club. ScoreAdjustment includes +10 (Community Boost) for those with community group. For U001: PrelimScore 720, adjustment +10 => adjusted 730, approved. U003: PrelimScore 695, +10 => 705, approved. U002: No community group, no adjustment, PrelimScore 710, rejected.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus bias: unprotected group gets favorable adjustment when belonging to certain community group, boosting scores leading to approval, while protected group never gets such boost.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Also LocalResident true for unprotected may indicate residency bias.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explain that bias manifests as systematic higher final scores for unprotected group members with community affiliation, leading to higher approval rates compared to protected group with same or higher raw scores.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We should note that protected group never receives adjustment, even if they had community group (none). So bias favoring unprotected group.
